metadata.title: Summits and Conferences in Current Affairs MCQs

questions.question: Where was 86th All India Presiding Officers Conference (AIPOC) 2026 held?
questions.explanation: The 86th All India Presiding Officers Conference was held in Lucknow at Uttar Pradesh Vidhan Sabha. The three-day conference (19-21 January) brings together presiding officers, secretaries, and representatives to discuss legislative procedures, parliamentary traditions, House conduct, good governance, and current legislative issues. Key objectives include leveraging technology for transparent, efficient, and citizen-centric legislative processes, capacity building of legislators to improve efficiency, strengthening democratic governance, and enhancing accountability of the legislature towards the public.

questions.question: The Cities of Care Conference was inaugurated at which Indian institute in February 2026?
questions.explanation: The Cities of Care Conference was inaugurated at IIT Madras Research Park by the University of Toronto India Foundation (UTIF), IIT Madras School of Sustainability, and University of Toronto School of Cities. The conference brings together global universities, research institutions, policymakers, and practitioners to advance climate action and sustainable urban development in India. The Sustainable India 2025 report, released during the conference with REVOLVE, highlights India’s progress and challenges in energy transition, waste management, urban resilience, and decarbonising industries.

questions.question: Which city is the venue of National State Election Commissioners (SECs) Conference 2026?
questions.explanation: Election Commission of India (ECI) will host the National Conference of State Election Commissioners (SECs) on 24 February 2026. The conference will be held at Bharat Mandapam in New Delhi after a gap of more than 25 years. The last National SEC Conference was held in 1999. State Election Commissioners from all States and Union Territories will attend along with legal and technical experts. Chief Electoral Officers of all 36 States and Union Territories will also participate. The aim is to build coordination between Election Commission of India (ECI) and State Election Commissioners (SECs) in electoral processes and logistics.

questions.question: What is the main theme of the National Conference on Naturopathy 2026?

options.label: D
options.text: Yoga and Naturopathy for a Stronger and Healthier You

answer.label: D
answer.text: Yoga and Naturopathy for a Stronger and Healthier You

questions.explanation: Eminent experts and researchers at the National Conference on Naturopathy–2026, held on 5–6 February 2026, stressed the need for evidence-based integration of Yoga and Naturopathy in preventive, promotive, and rehabilitative healthcare. The conference was jointly organised by the Morarji Desai National Institute of Yoga (MDNIY) and the Central Council for Research in Yoga & Naturopathy (CCRYN) under the Ministry of Ayush, Government of India. The main theme of the conference was “Yoga and Naturopathy for a Stronger and Healthier You”, with the sub-theme “SamYog” focusing on integrating Ayush systems with modern medicine for public health interventions.

questions.question: Where was National Conference on “Tackling Cyber-Enabled Frauds & Dismantling the Ecosystem” 2026 held?
questions.explanation: A two-day National Conference on “Tackling Cyber-Enabled Frauds & Dismantling the Ecosystem” has been inaugurated at Bharat Mandapam, New Delhi on 10 February 2026. The conference, organised by Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) with Indian Cyber Crime Coordination Centre (I4C), focuses on tackling cyber-enabled frauds involving finance, telecom misuse, and human exploitation. It brings together law enforcement, banks, regulators, technology platforms, and experts to strengthen coordination, technology use, and victim protection.

questions.question: Where was 23rd BioAsia 2026 international summit held?
questions.explanation: Telangana Chief Minister A. Revanth Reddy inaugurated the 23rd BioAsia 2026 international summit in Hyderabad. The two-day event is held at Hyderabad International Convention Centre (HICC). The theme for 2026 is “TechBio Unleashed – Artificial Intelligence (AI), Automation, and Revolutionary Changes in Life Sciences.” The summit highlights India’s rise as a global TechBio innovation hub. It features global scientists, industry leaders, and life sciences organisations.

questions.question: Which city is the venue for the National Round Table Conference of ECI and State Election Commissioners (SECs)?
questions.explanation: Election Commission of India (ECI) will hold National Round Table Conference with State Election Commissioners (SECs) on 24 February 2026 at Bharat Mandapam, New Delhi after 27 years; last held in 1999. It will be chaired by Chief Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar with Election Commissioners and participation of all States and Union Territories. The objective is to improve coordination between ECI and SECs within their legal frameworks and promote cooperative federalism. Discussions will cover sharing of technology, Electronic Voting Machines (EVMs) and Electoral Rolls.

questions.question: Which state/UT is the host of National Governance Conference 2026?
questions.explanation: Department of Administrative Reforms and Public Grievances with Government of Jammu and Kashmir organised the National Governance Conference on 3 March 2026 in Jammu. The conference was held in Jammu city of Jammu and Kashmir. The theme was “Holistic Development of Districts: Transforming Governance for Viksit Bharat”. It focused on improving district level governance and strengthening administration at grassroots level. The event highlighted innovative governance models and best practices from different districts across India.

questions.question: Where was 9th International Santal Conference held?
questions.explanation: The 9th International Santal Conference was held in Darjeeling, West Bengal on 7 March 2026. The conference focused on preservation of Santal language, culture, traditions and tribal heritage while encouraging social and economic development. It highlighted the historic struggles of the Santal community against exploitation, including the Santal Hul uprising of 1855. The event also recalled that the Santali language was included in the Eighth Schedule of the Constitution of India in 2003. It recognised the Ol Chiki script, invented in 1925, which helped strengthen Santali literature and cultural expression.

questions.question: Where was India–UK Conference on Green Hydrogen Standards and Safety Protocols held?
questions.explanation: The India–UK Conference on Green Hydrogen Standards and Safety Protocols was held on 27 February 2026 in New Delhi to strengthen cooperation on the safe deployment of green hydrogen under the National Green Hydrogen Mission. The conference was organised by the National Centre for Hydrogen Safety under the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy, in collaboration with the British High Commission in India and World Resources Institute India. It brought together representatives from government, industry, academia, standards bodies, testing institutions, research organisations, and regulatory agencies from India and the UK.
